title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Loss Of Female Libido
intro: Herbal teas for Loss of female libido are a type of herbal remedy used to address a decrease in sex drive in women, often caused by hormonal imbalances, stress, or menopause. These teas can be effective in treating this ailment due to their ability to balance hormones, reduce stress and anxiety, and increase blood flow to the genital area. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat loss of female libido include Maca root tea, which is believed to balance estrogen levels and increase desire; Damiana tea, which is said to increase blood flow and stimulate arousal; and Ginseng tea, which is thought to reduce stress and increase energy; as well as Ashwagandha tea, which is believed to reduce stress and anxiety and increase libido, and Wild yam tea, which is said to balance hormone levels and reduce symptoms of menopause. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Muira puama, Licorice root, and Red clover tea are also used to address this issue, each with unique properties and benefits that contribute to increased libido and overall women's health.
intro_study: According to "Advances in therapy", teas for loss of female libido may be beneficial as a natural alternative, as a study on a herbal formulation of Muira puama and Ginkgo biloba (Herbal vX) showed statistically significant improvements in various aspects of sex life in 65% of the sample after 1 month of treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido ailment, also known as hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD). The properties of this herbal preparation, including its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, help to alleviate stress and promote hormonal balance, which are essential for a healthy libido. The bioactive constituents of Panax ginseng, such as ginsenosides and eleutherosides, play a crucial role in stimulating the brain's reward system, increasing arousal, and enhancing overall sexual function. The benefits of using Panax ginseng teas to treat this ailment include improved mood, reduced stress levels, and increased energy, leading to a more satisfying and fulfilling sex life.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Panax ginseng root, and a tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Add the dried Panax ginseng root to the tea infuser and place it in the pot.
Steep the Panax ginseng root in the water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink it 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Panax ginseng tea daily for 3-4 weeks.
Start with 400-800mg of ginsenosides per cup, adjust as needed.
Take tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before meals or intimacy.
Monitor and adjust dosage based on individual response and libido improvement.
Consult a healthcare professional if libido does not improve within 6 weeks.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to treat the loss of female libido ailment, but its use may lead to side effects such as insomnia, anxiety, and increased heart rate due to its stimulating properties. Precautions when using Panax ginseng teas include avoiding excessive consumption, especially in women with a history of bleeding disorders or high blood pressure, as it may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido ailment due to their ability to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, including flavonoids and terpenoids, help to increase nitric oxide levels, leading to improved circulation and relaxation of blood vessels. This herbal preparation helps to treat the loss of female libido by increasing estrogen levels, reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting a healthy balance of neurotransmitters. The benefits of Ginkgo biloba teas for treating this ailment include improved libido, increased energy, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for women experiencing low libido.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy them at a health food store or online.
Combine 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the leaves to release their active ingredients.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea per day, ideally before bedtime, to support female libido.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Ginkgo biloba tea for libido loss.
Take 1 tablespoon of Ginkgo biloba tea leaves 2-3 times a day.
Steep the leaves in a cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea per day for 2-3 months to see results.
Monitor your libido levels and adjust the dosage as recommended by your doctor.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Advances in therapy", Ginkgo biloba teas for loss of female libido may have shown efficacy in improving various aspects of a woman's sex life, including frequency of sexual desires, intercourse, and fantasies, as well as satisfaction with sex life and ability to reach orgasm, based on a study involving 202 healthy women.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to potentially treat the loss of female libido, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and headaches in some individuals. To safely use Ginkgo biloba teas, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and also to avoid taking it with other medications or herbal supplements, and to be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to the potential dizziness and other side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Tribulus terrestris teas have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido, a condition often referred to as low sex drive. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as furostanol saponins, particularly protodioscin, which help to stimulate the production of testosterone and estrogen, thereby increasing sexual desire and arousal. By regulating hormonal imbalances and enhancing blood flow to the genital area, Tribulus terrestris teas can help to alleviate symptoms of low libido, promoting a healthier and more fulfilling sex life. The benefits of using Tribulus terrestris teas to treat low libido include increased energy, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for women seeking to revitalize their sex drive.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 2-3 grams of dried Tribulus terrestris root.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Tribulus terrestris root and add it to a tea infuser.
Steep the Tribulus terrestris root in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, preferably before meals, to help improve female libido.
preparation_usage: Start with 1/2 cup of Tribulus terrestris tea, 2-3 times a day.
Take 1 teaspoon of Tribulus terrestris extract 2-3 times a day.
Drink 1 cup of tea 30 minutes before meals, 2-3 times a day.
Increase dosage to 1 cup of tea or 2 teaspoons of extract after 2 weeks.
Use for 6-8 weeks, then take a 2-week break before resuming.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of biological regulators and homeostatic agents", Tribulus terrestris teas for loss of female libido may be beneficial due to the plant's potential aphrodisiac activity, which is attributed to its main active phytoconstituents including flavonoids, alkaloids, saponins, lignin, amides, and glycosides.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Tribulus terrestris teas can be used to potentially treat the loss of female libido, but it may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, blood pressure fluctuations, and interactions with certain medications like blood thinners and blood pressure medications. When using Tribulus terrestris teas to treat this ailment, it is essential to take precautions, such as drinking plenty of water, avoiding excessive caffeine consumption, and monitoring blood pressure and heart rate regularly.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, also known as oat straw tea, have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido due to its adaptogenic and nervine properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and balancing the body's hormonal system. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa teas, such as avenanthramides and avenacosides, have been found to have a positive effect on the hormonal balance, reducing cortisol levels and increasing the production of estrogen and progesterone. The benefits of Avena sativa teas in treating female libido loss include improved mood, reduced stress, and increased energy levels, making it a popular natural remedy for women experiencing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Avena sativa in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for a week to see effects.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Avena sativa tea.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried Avena sativa per cup of boiling water.
Drink 1 cup of Avena sativa tea 1-2 times a day for 1-2 weeks.
Increase dosage to 1 teaspoon per cup if libido doesn't improve after 2 weeks.
Monitor libido improvements and adjust dosage or consult a healthcare professional as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat the loss of female libido, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its sedative and relaxing properties. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming the tea before bedtime, and be cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery, as Avena sativa can impair concentration and reaction time.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the root of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat female libido disorders due to their adaptogenic and anti-stress properties. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to balance the body's hormonal system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating symptoms of low libido. preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. If using fresh roots, dry them in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Crush the Glycyrrhiza glabra roots into small pieces. Use a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder to get a fine powder.
Take 1 teaspoon of the powdered Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and add it to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce the heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals. Start with a small amount (1/2 cup) and adjust as needed.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea twice a day.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root daily.
Use tea for 8-12 weeks to notice improvements in libido.
Combine with a balanced diet and regular exercise for best results.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat the loss of female libido, but it may cause side effects such as high blood pressure, low potassium levels, and irregular heartbeat in some individuals due to its potential to interact with other medications. To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, take precautions such as consuming it in moderation, avoiding prolonged use, and monitoring your blood pressure and potassium levels regularly, especially if you have a history of heart or kidney conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitex agnus-castus teas have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido, also known as hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD). preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers or 2 cups of fresh flowers.

Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add the measured flowers to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers. Let it cool before drinking.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Vitex agnus-castus tea daily in the morning.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried herb in a tea infuser or a strainer.

Drink the tea for 2-3 months to notice improvements in libido.

preparations.preparation_study: According to "The journal of sexual medicine", Vitex agnus-castus teas may be recommended for the treatment of several female sexual dysfunctions, including loss of female libido, although there is a need for further clinical studies to confirm its efficacy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitex agnus-castus teas can be used to treat the loss of female libido, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its hormonal balancing properties. preparations.herb_desc: Dioscorea villosa teas have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido, also known as hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD). preparations.preparation_side_effect: Dioscorea villosa teas can be used to treat the loss of female libido ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and diarrhea. preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been found to effectively treat the loss of female libido ailment, also known as hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD). preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating the loss of female libido, however, some possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea or nausea, due to the high content of curcumin, a compound known to irritate the stomach lining. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to potentially treat female libido loss, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal disturbances, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido, also known as female sexual dysfunction, due to its adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing stress and anxiety, improving mood, and enhancing overall well-being, ultimately leading to increased interest in sexual activity. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis teas, including schisandrins, lignans, and polyphenols, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis and improving nitric oxide production, which plays a crucial role in female sexual arousal. The benefits of using Schisandra chinensis teas to treat female libido loss include improved mood, reduced stress, enhanced overall health, and increased interest in sexual activity, making it a potential natural remedy for addressing this common women's health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of Schisandra chinensis dried powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the Schisandra chinensis powder in a cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then stir well.
Strain the liquid into another cup to remove the powder.
Drink the tea immediately, ideally 2-3 times a week for best results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can potentially alleviate the loss of female libido, but may also cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications due to its active compounds like schisandrins and lignans. To use Schisandra chinensis teas safely, one should be cautious of its potential effects on pregnancy, breastfeeding, or blood pressure, and also ensure that it is consumed in moderation to avoid over-stimulation or other adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper methysticum teas, also known as kava, have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido ailment, also known as hypoactive sexual desire disorder. The anti-anxiety and sedative properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate stress and promote relaxation, thereby increasing libido and improving overall sexual well-being. The bioactive constituents of Piper methysticum teas, including kavalactones such as kawain and yangonin, have been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system and reproductive system, contributing to its therapeutic benefits. The benefits of using Piper methysticum teas to treat the loss of female libido ailment include reduced anxiety and stress, improved sleep quality, and enhanced sexual desire and satisfaction.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Piper methysticum root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a cup for tea.
Place the Piper methysticum root in the cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the root.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before sex to help increase female libido.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper methysticum teas can be used to potentially increase female libido, but it may also cause side effects such as drowsiness, confusion, and increased heart rate due to its high levels of kavalactones, which can interact with other medications or exacerbate pre-existing conditions. To minimize risks, one should start with a low dose, monitor their body's reaction closely, and be cautious when combining it with other substances, especially those that affect the central nervous system.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle teas, have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido ailment due to their estrogenic and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to decreased libido. The estrogenic properties of Silybum marianum teas help to stimulate the production of estrogen, a hormone that plays a crucial role in female reproductive health and libido. By regulating hormonal imbalances and reducing oxidative stress, Silybum marianum teas may help to alleviate symptoms of low libido in women, including decreased sexual desire and satisfaction.
preparation_procedure: Gather Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Use 1 cup of flowers and leaves for every 2 cups of boiling water.
Dry the Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. You can air-dry or use a food dehydrator.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the Silybum marianum in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid and discard the solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of Silybum marianum tea 2-3 times a day for a month to see results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat the loss of female libido, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. Precautions to take when using Silybum marianum teas include starting with a low dose, monitoring liver function, and avoiding consumption with other medications or substances that may interact with it.

preparations.herb_desc: Eurycoma longifolia teas, also known as Tongkat Ali, have been traditionally used to treat the loss of female libido ailment due to its aphrodisiac properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as eurycomanone and eurycomanoside, help to increase the levels of testosterone and improve mood, thereby enhancing female libido. The benefits of using Eurycoma longifolia teas include improved sexual function, increased energy, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition. By stimulating the body's natural response and promoting a sense of relaxation and well-being, Eurycoma longifolia teas can help to restore female libido and improve overall sexual health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Eurycoma longifolia powder and 1 cup of hot water.
Boil the water in a kettle or on a stove.
Pour the hot water into a cup and add the Eurycoma longifolia powder.
Stir the mixture well for 10-15 seconds.
Let the tea sit for 5-10 minutes before straining and drinking.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eurycoma longifolia teas can be used to potentially treat the loss of female libido, but possible side effects include headaches, dizziness, and stomach upset, as well as interactions with other medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Precautions to take when using Eurycoma longifolia teas for this purpose include avoiding excessive consumption, which can lead to increased heart rate and blood pressure, and being aware of potential allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching.
